title: CPI Shows Inflation Accelerating to 3.8% Annually in April After Weeks of War in Iran
summary: The Consumer Price Index rose 3.8% in April from a year earlier as higher energy costs replaced tariffs as the driver of higher prices for Americans.

title: Steel Tariffs Are Raising the Price of Canned Foods
summary: The cost to put food in cans remains high because manufacturers must still import steel from overseas. U.S. Steel plans to reopen a tin-plate factory.

title: Consumers Lean on a ‘Hamster Wheel’ of Credit to Manage Rising Costs
summary: As prices increase for gas, groceries and other staples, more and more households are borrowing to get by.

title: Oil Prices Rise on U.S.-Iran Standoff
summary: President Trump has declared the cease-fire on “life support,” and investors are taking a cautious approach as the economic effects of the war become more apparent.

title: How Iran’s Internet Blackout Is Crippling Its Economy, and the Fight Over Fruit-Flavored Vapes
summary: Plus, how everything became a subscription.

title: Xi Is Poised to Press Trump on Arms Sales to Taiwan During China Summit
summary: Beijing has called Taiwan the “core of China’s core interests.” Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.

title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
summary: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.

title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
summary: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.

title: China Sought Access to Anthropic’s Newest A.I. The Answer Was No.
summary: The latest artificial intelligence models from Anthropic and OpenAI are extending the United States’ lead over China and intensifying the rivalry between the countries.

title: As Trump Dismisses Iran Truce Plan, World Braces for Lasting Economic Pain
summary: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.

title: Who Owns the Strait?
summary: We take a look at the laws governing waterways.

title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
summary: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.

title: How to Eat Through Our List of the Best Restaurants in New York City
summary: Don’t have the time (or money) to try all 100? Here are a dozen bite-size bucket lists to inspire you.

title: Kari Lake, Who Led Effort to Shutter Voice of America, Is Named Ambassador to Jamaica
summary: Ms. Lake tried and failed to fire hundreds of Voice of America journalists and to cut funding for other federally funded news groups like Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.

title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.

title: Who Might Challenge Starmer in a Leadership Contest?
summary: Health Secretary Wes Streeting, Mayor of Greater Manchester Andy Burnham and former deputy prime minister Angela Rayner are among the possible contenders.

title: Russia Keeps Attacking U.S. Firms in Ukraine. The White House Is Silent.
summary: Facilities tied to Coca-Cola, Cargill, Mondelez and others appear to have been deliberately hit. The Trump administration’s muted response has raised concerns.

title: The War in Iran Is Another Blow to Russia’s Credibility. Or Is It?
summary: Despite its long slog in Ukraine and the loss of former allies like Syria, Russia is benefiting from costly energy, a clever narrative and America’s own failure to achieve a quick victory.

title: Former Congressman Barney Frank, Now in Hospice, Offers Democrats Frank Words of Advice
summary: Mr. Frank speaks about the missteps of the Democratic Party and his hope for its future. “Frankly, if I weren’t dying, people wouldn’t be paying as much attention.”

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
summary: Democrats will choose a candidate in a House battleground in Nebraska, and an unusual Senate primary could decide the fate of Omaha’s “blue dot” vote.

title: Why So Many Guys Are Obsessed With Testosterone
summary: From the Trump administration to online influencers, the hormone is increasingly seen as the key to achieving a new male ideal.

title: What to Know About the New Obsession With Testosterone
summary: From politics to influencers and beyond, the hormone is being used not just for medical reasons but in pursuit of a new masculine ideal.

title: Wrongful Death Lawsuits Against OpenAI Test a New Strategy
summary: The cases seek to use consumer product safety laws to rein in chatbot companies.

title: Start-Up Raises $1.3 Billion for an A.I. ‘Grid’
summary: As tech giants dominate the hardware needed to build artificial intelligence, Amp hopes to create an alternative.

title: Is France’s Centrist Leader Trying to Weatherproof It From the Far Right?
summary: President Emmanuel Macron has appointed a string of allies who will remain in critical state positions after next year’s presidential election, and who could thwart a far-right successor.

title: Macron Makes a Bid for New Partners in Africa
summary: The French president visited Kenya this week for a summit designed to build stronger ties with Anglophone African countries.

title: Did Epstein Actually Write That Suicide Note? Here’s How We Tried to Find Out.
summary: Three New York Times reporters discuss the steps they have taken to learn more about a note found in Jeffrey Epstein’s jail cell in the weeks before his death.

title: Altar Boy Who Called Out Sexual Abuse in the Philippines Is Now a Lawyer for Victims
summary: Michal Gatchalian dared to speak out against sexual abuse by a priest in the devoutly Catholic Philippines. He’s now a lawyer helping other victims.

title: The Shared Feeling of Being Harvested by the Future
summary: There is a shared sense of precarity that lies beneath the envy and distrust.

title: We Should Be Taking Hantavirus More Seriously
summary: There are too many unknowns to be certain about much.

title: Stephen Colbert Hosts 4 of His ‘Best Television Friends’
summary: Jimmy Kimmel, Jimmy Fallon, John Oliver and Seth Meyers, co-hosts of the Strike Force Five podcast along with Colbert, joined as guests on what is likely their last appearance together on the “Late Show.”

title: Instructure Strikes Deal for Hackers for Return of Canvas Data
summary: Instructure, which provides Canvas software to thousands of schools and universities around the world, did not say what it had given the hackers in exchange for the stolen data.

title: EBay Rejects GameStop’s $55 Billion Takeover Bid
summary: The online marketplace called the cash-and-stock proposal “neither credible nor attractive.”

title: Texas Rainbow Crosswalk Ban Leads San Antonio to Paint Sidewalks Instead
summary: After the state ordered the removal of rainbow crosswalks, San Antonio used colorful sidewalks to keep public pride symbols alive in the city.

title: Rex Reed, Film Critic Known for Acerbic Reviews, Dies at 87
summary: He fawned over Old Hollywood stars and sparred with Frank Sinatra. Nora Ephron marveled at his ability to get his subjects to say the things they did.
